headline_summary: Iran threatens to attack any ship passing through the Strait of Hormuz.
U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran escalate tensions in the region.
Five ships have been hit in a wave of attacks in the Gulf.
Shipping giants suspend operations through the Strait of Hormuz.
Oil prices are expected to surge due to supply disruptions.
The Strait of Hormuz is critical for global oil and gas supplies.
Insurance companies reassess war-risk exposure in the region.
Houthi militants signal renewed attacks on shipping in the Red Sea.

Energy: Oil prices could spike to $200 per barrel.
Natural gas prices rose sharply in Europe and Asia.
Disruptions in oil supply could lead to inflationary pressures globally.

risk_drivers: Escalating military conflict in the Middle East.
Threats to critical maritime trade routes.
Potential for significant disruptions in oil supply.

watchlist.why: Expected price surge due to supply disruptions.
Suspension of operations through the Strait of Hormuz.
